'''Carole Grawley''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Farmer/Legends|farmer]] from the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] who was the [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] of [[Grif Grawley|Grif]] and the mother of [[Katie Grawley]].<ref name="jk" /> 

==History==
Carole was a [[Sulon/Legends|Sulonese]] [[gender/Legends|woman]] who knew [[Katarn family]]. She married Grif Grawley and they had a daughter, [[Katie Grawley|Katie]].<ref name="jk" /> 

In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Grawley and Carole were outside their house and bore witness to the [[Sulon massacre]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] firsthand. They first saw an Imperial [[probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] passing near their home, and then a series of [[blaster/Legends|blaster]] shots tore through and set their house on fire. In an attempt to rescue Katie, who cried desperately for help, Carole bolted toward the home; Grawley barely pulled her back before the structure collapsed, which killed their daughter. But not before Carole been severely burned which left prominent scars on her face.<ref name="jk">''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]''</ref>

Afterward with the help of [[Morgan Katarn]] the couple emigrated to [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]].<ref name="jk" /> 

One time around [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], on the eve of Katie's birthday, her husband "celebrated" by burying his grief with [[alcohol/Legends|alcohol]] in [[Smuggler's Rest (Ruusan)|Smuggler's Rest]] until he fell unconscious. She was summoned to [[Fort Nowhere]], and with the help of some locals, they loaded him on their [[skimmer/Legends|skimmer]] to return home.<ref name="jk" />

Six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, Grif was near [[Hill 461]] herding their [[gra]] and called her for good night, when Carol notified him that all [[weather satellite]]s were off-line. He failed returning the next day and worried, she called [[Horley]] to Fort Nowhere to ask his whereabouts.<ref name="jk" /><ref name="jk audio">[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]]</ref>

Eventually Grif returned home and his fears proved right, as an [[assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]] bearing Imperial representatives landed in the settlement. Carole heard that they only talked with Mayor [[Byron Devo III]] and then saw the shuttle leaving without any trouble, and tried to quiet Grif's worries; she hoped that after the death of the Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] the [[Imperial Remnant]] changed. But she didn't change Grif's mind, who insisted to leave their home.<ref name="jk audio" />

Indeed, soon after Grif was proven right and despite their initial moves, the Imperials [[Battle of Fort Nowhere|devastated]] [[Fort Nowhere]]. Grif managed to gather some survivors and they all hid in an [[Unidentified Ruusan temple|ancient temple]]. Not long after, an [[Moldy Crow/Legends|unknown ship]], following the direction transmitted by Grif, reached a pinpointed location, and [[Fido]] inspected it and the pilots, and led them to the temple; monitoring Fido's footage, she wondered if they were Rebels, and Grif wondered to her if one of the pilots was [[Kyle Katarn]], Morgan's son. Once the ship arrived, it turned out that it was indeed Kyle. Meeting Grif, he asked about his wife, to which Carole claimed she was okay, and thanked his father for this, explaining that it was he who brought them on that planet. She was shocked later when she learned that Morgan had died, and it was the reason for Kyle to defect from the Empire.<ref name="jk" /><ref name="jk audio" />

Inside the temple, Carole heard her husband narrating again the recent developments, events that she would never forget. She lamented that all these were caused because the townspeople did not pay attention to Grif's warnings. She later heard Katarn's story about his defection from the Empire, the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] who [[Search for the Valley of the Jedi|is after]] the [[Valley of the Jedi]] on the planet, and [[Seven Dark Jedi|his minions]] who attacked them. Carole found his narration incredible in her enthusiasm, although [[Luther Pardy]], thought they were "fairy tales", and accused Katarn for being a [[spy/Legends|spy]].<ref name="jk" /><ref name="jk audio" />

A few days later her husband accompanied the agents on their quest to the Valley of the Jedi, but never returned, as he lost his life while battling an [[AD-43|attack droid]], somewhere outside Jerec's base.<ref name="jk" />

==Personality and traits==
Carole considered her husband Grif tactless. After his drinking incident she did not approve drinking much [[Old Trusty]].<ref name="jk" /> 

Being closely attached to him, she disliked when he had to herd the [[gra]]s in long range, and was particularly worried whenever he drove their old skimmer. She didn't like the idea of [[cave farming]].<ref name="jk audio" />

The moments after the destruction of the Fort, and how her outcast husband agreed to help and became the leader of the survivors was a real miracle to her.<ref name="jk" /> 

==Behind the scenes==
In the audio adaptation of [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'']], she was voiced by [[Sue Scott]].

==Appearances==
